data: lk-data-pack-roundtrip.description: Use for network packet construction and binary save-data blobs. pack serialises Lua values using a format string; unpack reverses it. crc32 detects corruption on receive before you call unpack.
body: -- unpack (validate crc before this in real code):
local id, value, name = d.unpack(">Hfz", payload)
print(string.format("id=%d  value=%.2f  name=%s", id, value, name))

data: lk-data-pack-struct-batch.description: Use to serialise a homogeneous array of fixed-size records (vectors, transform matrices, physics states) into one compact binary blob. getPackedSize verifies expected sizes without doing a full pack.
body: blob = blob .. d.pack(FMT, v[1], v[2], v[3])

data: lk-data-write-read.description: Use newWriter when building a binary payload incrementally (message framing, file chunk headers). Typed methods writeU8/writeU16LE/writeF32LE are safer than raw pack for variable-length record construction.
body: -- read back the first two fields with the format-string API:

data: lk-data-dataview-inspect.description: Use DataView to inspect a binary blob received from a file or network without copying it. Reads typed values at arbitrary byte offsets — useful for parsing file-format headers or network message envelopes.
body: -- simulate a 12-byte header: magic(4), version(2), data_len(4)
print(string.format("magic=0x%08x  ver=%d  data_len=%d", magic, version, dlen))

data: lk-data-bytedata-cipher.description: Use LByteData for in-place byte manipulation: simple ciphers, checksum accumulation, or binary patching. newByteData(str) copies bytes in; getByte/setByte let you iterate and modify individual bytes.

data: lk-data-hash-asset-key.description: Use hash() to derive a compact, reproducible lookup key from an asset path or chunk content. Use "sha256" for integrity checks; "xxh64" for fast non-security cache keys (~4x faster).

data: lk-data-crc32-save.description: Use to protect save files and transmitted blobs from silent corruption. Append the CRC to the blob on write; recompute and compare on load before parsing the payload.
body: local save = d.encodeToml({ player = { level = 5, gold = 300 } })
-- on load: strip last 4 bytes and verify

data: lk-data-base64.description: Use to embed binary data in JSON strings, URLs, or text protocols. encode("base64") produces the RFC 4648 string; decode("base64") reverses it back to raw bytes.

data: lk-data-compress-save.description: Use zlib compression on save blobs to reduce file-system write size. Level 6 balances speed and ratio for structured config data; verify round-trip before writing with the assert check below.
body: local payload    = d.encodeToml({ world = { map = "dungeon_01", seed = 42 } })

data: lk-data-compress-chunks.description: Use compressChunks for streaming serialisation of large maps or level sections split into tiles. Pass a table of binary strings to avoid allocating one giant concatenated blob before compression.

data: lk-data-toml-load.description: Use at startup to load a TOML config, apply defaults for optional keys, and validate required fields. Fail fast with a clear error rather than silently propagating nil values into game logic.
body: assert(type(volume) == "number" and volume >= 0 and volume <= 1, "invalid volume")

data: lk-data-toml-patch.description: Use to apply partial preference updates without rewriting the whole config. parseToml -> mutate the table -> encodeToml preserves all other keys and structure.

data: lk-data-toml-manifest.description: Use to load and validate a game manifest (name, version, entry point) during boot. Centralise all schema checks here so the game loop never receives unvalidated manifest data.

data: lk-data-ringbuffer-queue.description: Use as a bounded event queue for combat log messages, input history, or debug entries. The buffer overwrites the oldest entry when full — no manual eviction code required.

data: lk-data-ringbuffer-rolling-avg.description: Use a ring buffer to compute a rolling average of frame times, damage-per-second, or sensor readings. toTable() yields the current window as a plain Lua array for sum/count without a separate counter.
body: for _, v in ipairs(samples) do sum = sum + v end

data: lk-data-msgpack-roundtrip.description: Use toMsgPack / fromMsgPack for compact structured serialisation of game state snapshots passed between Lua VMs via Channel, or written as save slot blobs. Smaller than TOML; more type-safe than raw pack.

engine: lk-engine-version-guard.description: Use at the very top of main.lua to assert a minimum engine version before any other code runs. Prevents silent breakage when players ship or run an older binary without rebuilding.

engine: lk-engine-platform-branch.description: Use when you need platform-specific paths: keybind hint text, default save directories, cursor behaviour, or audio driver selection. Cache at module level — avoid calling platform() every frame.

engine: lk-engine-debug-only-block.description: Use to gate ALL expensive debug overlays, cheat commands, and verbose assertion dumps. In release, isDebug() returns false so this block is skipped entirely — no runtime cost.
body: -- god mode keybind, profiler HUD, overlay text, verbose log

engine: lk-engine-fps-warn.description: Use in update() to detect sustained low-FPS before players notice stutter. Derives the warning threshold from the actual frame budget so the check stays valid across 30 / 60 / 120 FPS targets.
body: local warn_fps  = (1000 / budget_ms) * 0.75   -- 75 % of target FPS
print(string.format("[perf] fps=%.1f below warning=%.1f", fps, warn_fps))

engine: lk-engine-even-odd-stagger.description: Use to split expensive per-frame work across alternating frames (AI ticks on even, particle updates on odd) without extra boolean state variables. frameCount() is the canonical engine clock — safe to use in save data.
body: -- AI tick, physics LOD, ambient sound probe

engine: lk-engine-uptime-stamp.description: Use for session analytics, auto-save triggers, and achievement timers. uptime() is wall-clock seconds since engine start — combine with frameCount() to attach a reproducible stamp to save records and bug reports.
body: local stamp    = string.format("t=%dm%02ds  f=%d", minutes, seconds, lurek.engine.frameCount())

engine: lk-engine-fixed-dt.description: Use for physics / simulation that needs deterministic fixed steps. Drives the accumulator loop with uptime delta; clamps the spiral-of-death so a slow machine can't accumulate hundreds of ticks on one spike frame.
body: -- call this each frame from your update() callback:
local delta = math.min(now - _last_t, 0.25)  -- spiral-of-death cap = 250 ms

engine: lk-engine-config-hotreload.description: Use when supporting hot-reload of conf.toml at runtime. Compare the config revision each frame; if it changed, re-read and re-apply settings without restarting the game.

engine: lk-engine-memory-hud.description: Use for a single-line memory HUD. Combines lua_kb + resource stats into one formatted string suitable for lurek.render.print() in a debug overlay.
body: local hud = string.format("lua=%d KB  res=%.1f/%.0f MB", mem.lua_kb, res_mb, budget_mb)

engine: lk-engine-resource-budget-init.description: Use once in main.lua or level_init() to set the memory ceiling for all loaded assets. Prevents unbounded accumulation during level streaming. Pick a value ≤ 50 % of target device RAM to leave headroom for OS + GPU.
body: print(string.format("[init] resource budget=%.0f MB total=%.1f MB",

engine: lk-engine-pressure-abort-load.description: Use before streaming a large asset batch (level, cutscene). Abort the load early and trigger a cache purge if utilisation is already above 85 %, rather than crashing mid-load with an OOM.
body: error(string.format("resource pressure %.0f%% — purge cache before loading level", ratio * 100))
print(string.format("[load] pressure=%.0f%%, ok to proceed", ratio * 100))

engine: lk-engine-frame-profile.description: Use during optimisation sprints to print a structured per-phase breakdown each frame. More actionable than fps() alone — shows which phase (lua, render, audio) is the bottleneck.

engine: lk-engine-profile-overlay.description: Use as a single-line debug overlay. getFrameProfileText() returns a pre-formatted multi-line string — pass it straight to render.print().

engine: lk-engine-spike-log.description: Use to detect and log individual spike frames without flooding the console. Only prints profile text when the frame exceeds 2× the configured budget.

engine: lk-engine-boot-record.description: Use as the very first call in main.lua. Emits one reproducible boot record that correlates crash reports with the exact binary, platform, and memory state at launch.
body: "[boot] ver=%s  platform=%s  debug=%s  budget_ms=%d  lua_kb=%d  res_budget_mb=%.0f",

input: lk-input-kb-move.description: Use in update() to produce a normalised 2-axis movement vector from WASD + arrow keys. Works for top-down, platformer, and grid games. Result is clamped to [-1, 1] so diagonal speed does not exceed 1.
body: local dx = (kb.isDown("d", "right") and 1 or 0) - (kb.isDown("a", "left") and 1 or 0)
local dy = (kb.isDown("s", "down")  and 1 or 0) - (kb.isDown("w", "up")   and 1 or 0)
local len = math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
if len > 0 then dx, dy = dx / len, dy / len end
print("move axis dx=" .. dx .. " dy=" .. dy)

input: lk-input-kb-modifier.description: Use to detect hotkeys that require a modifier (Ctrl+S save, Shift+Tab, Alt+F4 intercept). Combine isDown for the primary key with isModifierActive for the modifier — avoids false positives from key order.
body: if ctrl_s  then print("save triggered") end
if shift_z then print("redo triggered") end

input: lk-input-kb-text.description: Use when opening a chat box, search field, or rename dialog. Enables OS text-input mode (IME, dead keys, key repeat) and disables it on close. Pair with setKeyRepeat so held backspace deletes characters continuously.

input: lk-input-kb-scancode-hint.description: Use to show localised key names in tooltip / rebind UI. Scancode gives physical position; getKeyFromScancode gives the printable character on the player's actual keyboard layout (AZERTY, QWERTZ, etc.).

input: lk-input-mouse-world.description: Use to map screen-space mouse position to world-space coordinates by subtracting the camera offset. Foundation for click-to-move, hover highlight, and drag-select in top-down games.
body: -- subtract camera offset (replace with your cam_x / cam_y variables)
local wx, wy = mx + cam_x, my + cam_y
mx, my, wx, wy, tostring(left_click), tostring(right_click)))

input: lk-input-mouse-zoom.description: Use for scroll-wheel zoom in map or level-editor views. Accumulates wheel delta and clamps to a [min, max] range each frame to prevent jump flicker.
body: zoom = math.max(zoom_min, math.min(zoom_max, zoom + wheel * ZOOM_SPEED))

input: lk-input-mouse-cursor-ctx.description: Use to change the cursor shape based on context: arrow when idle, hand when hovering a button, crosshair in targeting mode. Uses system cursors — no image files required.
body: -- each frame: swap based on state
local hovering_button = false  -- replace with your hover logic

input: lk-input-mouse-fps-look.description: Use for first-person or third-person camera rotation. Relative mode disables OS cursor movement and reports raw deltas — essential for smooth 360° look. Disable it when showing a pause menu.
body: print("fps look mode active, relative=" .. tostring(mouse.getRelativeMode()))
-- in your look update: read delta (engine delivers via events)

input: lk-input-mouse-drag-box.description: Use for rubber-band selection in strategy or editor UIs. Tracks drag start on left-button press and computes the selection rectangle while held.
body: if mouse.isDown(1) and not drag_active then
local rx = math.min(drag_start_x or cx, cx)
local ry = math.min(drag_start_y or cy, cy)
print(string.format("drag rect=%.0f,%.0f %.0fx%.0f", rx, ry, rw, rh))

input: lk-input-gp-deadzone.description: Use to read left analogue stick with a circular deadzone. Raw axis values drift near 0 on all controllers — apply deadzone before using for movement to prevent ghost input.
body: local len     = math.sqrt(raw_x * raw_x + raw_y * raw_y)

input: lk-input-gp-button-edge.description: Use to detect single-frame button press and release edges for jump, attack, and UI confirm/cancel. wasPressed fires only on the first frame the button goes down; wasReleased fires only on the frame it goes up.

input: lk-input-gp-dpad.description: Use for menu navigation with D-pad. virtualDpad converts left-stick x/y plus deadzone threshold into a discrete direction string — works for both real D-pad (hat) and analogue stick input in the same code path.
body: local dpad = gp.virtualDpad(sx, sy, 0.5)   -- threshold=0.5 avoids accidental triggers
if dpad.up    then print("menu: up")    end
if dpad.down  then print("menu: down")  end
if dpad.left  then print("menu: left")  end
if dpad.right then print("menu: right") end

input: lk-input-gp-hat.description: Use when the game needs raw hat/D-pad state (not stick emulation) for fighting-game inputs or precise direction detection. getHat returns a direction string: "c", "u", "d", "l", "r", "ul", "ur", "dl", "dr".
body: if hat == "u" or hat == "ul" or hat == "ur" then
elseif hat == "d" or hat == "dl" or hat == "dr" then

input: lk-input-gp-connect.description: Use to detect controller hot-plug and show a UI notification. wasConnected/wasDisconnected are single-frame edge signals — check all slots each frame to handle multi-controller sessions.

input: lk-input-gp-vibrate.description: Use for damage-feedback or hit-confirmation rumble. Checks vibration support before calling to avoid silent no-ops on hardware that lacks motors. Pass different low/high values to distinguish damage type (heavy vs light).
body: -- heavy hit: strong low-frequency + light high-frequency, 300 ms
print("vibrate: hit feedback sent to pad " .. pad)

input: lk-input-gp-mapping.description: Use on startup to load a community controller mapping database so obscure gamepads are recognised as standard buttons/axes. The file is typically bundled with your game as content/gamecontrollerdb.txt.
body: print("  pad " .. pad .. " '" .. gp.getName(pad) .. "' guid=" .. gp.getGUID(pad))

input: lk-input-gp-full-poll.description: Use for a debug diagnostics panel that shows all axes and buttons for the first connected gamepad. Useful when verifying new controller mappings.

math: lk-math-smooth-follow.description: Use for camera tracking, health-bar animation, and any value that should ease toward a target without overshooting. smoothstep gives a natural velocity curve; speed is controlled by the lerp factor.
body: local speed   = 0.1   -- fraction per frame (higher = faster)

math: lk-math-progress-remap.description: Use to drive a progress window animation (e.g. show progress bar only between t=0.2 and t=0.8 of a full cutscene). remap + clamp extracts a sub-range of a normalised [0..1] timeline into its own [0..1].
body: local total_t  = 1.0   -- full timeline length (seconds or normalised)

math: lk-math-aim-distance.description: Use to compute the angle and distance between an actor and a target for aiming, aggro-range checks, and steering. angleBetween gives the heading in radians; distanceSq avoids the sqrt for range-only comparisons.
body: local angle  = m.angleBetween(px, py, tx, ty)
local dist   = m.distance(px, py, tx, ty)
local dist2  = m.distanceSq(px, py, tx, ty)

math: lk-math-inv-lerp-opacity.description: Use to compute a fade-in opacity from a distance or any ranged value. inverseLerp turns the raw measurement back into [0..1], then clamp prevents negative or >1 values at the extremes.
body: local dist      = 150      -- current distance to camera or trigger
local fade_near = 80       -- fully visible at or below this distance
local fade_far  = 200      -- fully invisible at or above this distance

math: lk-math-sign-bounce.description: Use for wall-bounce logic, score differentials, and left/right split. sign() returns -1, 0, or +1 — multiply by a speed to flip velocity on impact without a manual if/else branch.
body: local px_after = wall_x + 5   -- position just past the wall
-- flip velocity on the frame the object crosses the boundary
if m.sign(px_after - wall_x) ~= m.sign((px_after - vx) - wall_x) then

math: lk-math-closest-point-path.description: Use to snap an entity to the nearest point on a road, rail, or patrol path defined as polyline segments. Iterate segments and keep the closest result from closestPointOnSegment.
body: local cx, cy = m.closestPointOnSegment(px, py, ax, ay, bx, by)
local d      = m.distanceSq(px, py, cx, cy)

math: lk-math-laser-bounce.description: Use for laser / light-ray bouncing off a wall segment. lineIntersect returns the crossing point; segmentIntersectsSegment guards against infinite-line false positives outside segment extents.
body: local hit, ix, iy = m.segmentIntersectsSegment(rx1, ry1, rx2, ry2, wx1, wy1, wx2, wy2)
if hit and ix and iy then
print(string.format("laser hits wall at (%.1f,%.1f)", ix, iy))

math: lk-math-circle-overlap.description: Use for simple trigger zones, pickup radii, and explosion hit detection. circleContainsPoint for point-in-circle, circleIntersectsCircle for collision between two radial actors.
body: local player_in_zone = m.circleContainsPoint(zx, zy, zr, px, py)
local circles_touch  = m.circleIntersectsCircle(zx, zy, zr, px, py, pr)

math: lk-math-point-in-room.description: Use to test whether a character or cursor is inside an irregular room polygon — for room entry events, valid placement checks, and fog-of-war.
body: print(string.format("  area=%.1f  centroid=(%.1f,%.1f)", area, cx, cy))

math: lk-math-convex-hull.description: Use when building a selection boundary around a set of scattered units or points — the convex hull is the tightest non-concave enclosure and is also the correct input for physics shape decomposition.
body: -- scatter of unit positions (flat x,y pairs)

math: lk-math-triangulate.description: Use to decompose a concave room or level-section polygon into triangles before passing to lurek.render mesh calls or collision shape builders that require triangle lists.

math: lk-math-bresenham-grid.description: Use for tile-based line-of-sight, projectile paths on a grid, or highlighting cells a line passes through. bresenham returns all integer cell coordinates between two grid positions.

math: lk-math-rect-union.description: Use to compute the bounding box that covers multiple objects — for camera framing, dirty region invalidation, or multi-selection outlines.
body: -- grow bounding box over a list of objects
local bx, by, bw, bh = objects[1].x, objects[1].y, objects[1].w, objects[1].h
bx, by, bw, bh = m.rectUnion(bx, by, bw, bh, o.x, o.y, o.w, o.h)
print(string.format("bounding box=%.0f,%.0f %.0fx%.0f", bx, by, bw, bh))

math: lk-math-bezier-traj.description: Use for arcing projectile paths, thrown objects, or animated spline motion. Define start/control/end, then evaluate at t to get position each frame. Derivative curve gives the tangent for rotation of the sprite along the path.
body: local t  = 0.5   -- animation progress [0..1], advance by dt/total_time each frame
print(string.format("projectile at (%.1f,%.1f) t=%.2f", px, py, t))

math: lk-math-bezier-edit.description: Use in an in-game spline editor or patrol-path tool. Allow the designer to drag control points at runtime; evaluate + render to preview the curve instantly after each change.
body: -- simulate moving control point 2 to a new position:
-- render the updated curve as line segments

math: lk-math-catmullrom-patrol.description: Use for smooth patrol paths, camera dolly tracks, or any looping motion that must pass through exact waypoints. Catmull-Rom guarantees C1 continuity at every point — no sharp corners.
body: local t   = 0.0   -- advance by dt / patrol_duration each frame

math: lk-math-random-loot.description: Use for loot tables, ability proc chances, and random event triggers. random() returns [0,1); compare against a threshold for probability checks.

render: lk-render-debug-hud.description: Use for a semi-transparent debug card overlay. Combines a filled rectangle backdrop, setColor to reset alpha, and printf for aligned text.

render: lk-render-stats-overlay.description: Use when profiling draw call budget. getStats() shows drawcalls, textures, and GPU calls in one struct — displays a compact line each frame.

render: lk-render-additive-burst.description: Use for glowing particles, explosions, and energy beams. Additive blend mode accumulates light values — overlapping translucent circles brighten the area instead of replacing it.

render: lk-render-bg-color-transition.description: Use for day/night cycle, scene mood shift, or level transition. Lerps background RGB between two states each frame using a normalised progress t.
body: local t    = 0.5   -- [0=dawn, 1=dusk] — advance with uptime / cycle_duration

render: lk-render-camera-follow.description: Use as the outer push/pop wrapper around your scene draw. Translates the origin so the camera target stays centred on screen. Wrap all world-space draw calls inside this block.
body: -- replace with your actual camera target and smoothed cam_x/cam_y
local cam_x, cam_y = 320, 240   -- smoothed camera world position
r.circle("fill", cam_x, cam_y, 10)  -- actor at camera center

render: lk-render-camera-shake.description: Use for screen-shake on explosions, damage, and impacts. Applies a per-frame random offset capped by a decaying trauma value, then restores the matrix with pop() so UI elements are unaffected.
body: local trauma = 0.8   -- [0..1], decay toward 0 over time
local shake  = trauma * trauma * 12   -- quadratic falloff feels more natural
-- draw all world-space content inside here
-- decay trauma each frame: trauma = math.max(0, trauma - dt * 2)

render: lk-render-sprite-transform.description: Use to draw a sprite with its own local pivot, rotation, and scale without affecting subsequent draw calls. Essential for hierarchical character rigs (weapon attached to hand) or world-space billboards.
body: local wx, wy, angle, sx, sy = 300, 250, math.pi / 6, 2, 2
local ox, oy = W * 0.5, H * 0.5    -- pivot at image centre

render: lk-render-scissor-clip.description: Use to restrict rendering to a minimap frame, inventory slot, or scrolling text panel — nothing drawn outside the scissor rect appears on screen.
body: local vp_x, vp_y, vp_w, vp_h = 650, 10, 140, 140   -- minimap rectangle
r.rectangle("fill", vp_x, vp_y, vp_w, vp_h)    -- minimap background

render: lk-render-layer-setup.description: Use at game init to define named draw layers in z-order. All subsequent setLayer() calls use these names — easier to read than raw z numbers and safe to reorder without touching every draw site.

render: lk-render-draw-layer-priority.description: Use when you need fine-grained z-ordering within a layer (e.g. sort actors by Y position for 2.5D overlap). newDrawLayer queues callbacks by priority integer and flushes them in sorted order.

render: lk-render-canvas-post.description: Use to render a scene to an off-screen canvas, then draw the canvas to screen with a tint, scale, or custom shader — foundation for minimap thumbnails, reflection captures, and simple post-processing effects.
body: -- draw result with a blue tint at half size

render: lk-render-sprite-batch-tile.description: Use to render a tile map efficiently. SpriteBatch batches hundreds of quads in a single GPU draw call — essential for performance at 64×64+ tile maps. Rebuild the batch only when the map changes, not every frame.
body: -- build tile map (call once or when map changes)
local q = r.newQuad(0, 0, TILE, TILE, sw, sh)

render: lk-render-draw-many.description: Use for a pool of transient visual objects (sparks, dust motes, debris). Build the list table once per frame and pass to drawMany — one call batches all draw operations with different positions and rotations.
body: particles[i] = { img, px, py, ang, sc, sc }

render: lk-render-quad-anim.description: Use for frame-by-frame sprite animation from a sprite sheet. Build quads once at init, then select the current frame by index each update.
body: quads[i + 1] = r.newQuad(i * FRAME_W, 0, FRAME_W, FRAME_H, sw, sh)
-- in update(dt): anim_timer = anim_timer + dt; if anim_timer >= frame_duration then ...
r.draw(sheet, 300, 300)   -- placeholder: real usage: r.drawq(sheet, quads[current_frame], x, y)

render: lk-render-gradient-banner.description: Use for title screens, section headers, or UI banners. drawGradientRect creates a smooth horizontal fade; printf centres the text on top.

render: lk-render-hex-grid.description: Use for hex-grid strategy or board game views. Iterates a grid of pointy- top hex tiles with the standard offset stagger between columns.
body: local hy = 60 + row * (RADIUS * 2) + (col % 2 == 1 and RADIUS or 0)

render: lk-render-iso-cube.description: Use to draw isometric cube walls and floors. Vary topColor / leftColor / rightColor to encode terrain elevation, shadow, or material type while keeping a consistent isometric projection.

render: lk-render-bevel-panel.description: Use for retro / classic UI panels and inset fields. drawBevelRect provides raised, sunken, groove, and ridge effects with a single call — no extra image assets needed.

render: lk-render-rich-text.description: Use for status messages, chat lines, or dialogue that mix multiple colours and sizes in one string. printRich accepts a span table — no manual x offset bookkeeping required.

render: lk-render-wireframe.description: Use during collision-mesh debugging to switch all filled shapes to outline mode for one frame, then restore normal fill — revealing triangle topology without permanently changing draw calls.

system: lk-sys-platform-branch.description: Use to apply platform-specific code paths (e.g. keyboard shortcuts, path separators, OS integration). Combine with getArch() for more precise dispatch (e.g. "Windows" + "x86_64").

system: lk-sys-version-guard.description: Use at game boot to verify the engine version meets the minimum requirement. Fail fast with a clear message rather than crashing later on a missing API or changed behaviour.
body: if maj < 0 or (maj == 0 and min < 6) then
error("This game requires Lurek2D >= 0.6, found " .. ver)

system: lk-sys-info-dump.description: Use during development or debug builds to log the full engine environment (OS, version, Lua runtime, renderer, CPU count, memory). Gate behind isDebug to avoid leaking info in release builds.

system: lk-sys-env-path.description: Use to read environment variables for configuring dev overrides, save directories, or tool paths without hardcoding. Falls back gracefully when the variable is not set.

system: lk-sys-parseargs.description: Use to parse --flag and --key=value CLI arguments at game startup. parseArgs returns structured flags, options, and positional args so you can override config values without editing files.
body: local parsed = rt.parseArgs()   -- uses process args by default

system: lk-sys-log.description: Use lurek.runtime.log() for structured game-level log entries instead of print(). Level-tagged messages integrate with the RUST_LOG filter so debug lines are suppressed in release builds automatically.
body: rt.log("warn",  "Save slot 3 missing — creating new save")

system: lk-sys-loglevel.description: Use setLogLevel to lower verbosity in release builds and raise it during development sessions. Pair with getLogLevel() to display the current level in a debug overlay or settings menu.

system: lk-sys-clipboard.description: Use for user-facing copy/paste: share codes, map seeds, chat messages. getClipboardText returns nil when the clipboard is empty or unavailable — always guard before using the value.
body: -- write to clipboard (e.g. player copies a share code)
-- read back (e.g. player pastes a code from another window)

system: lk-sys-config-inspect.description: Use to read active engine config at runtime and detect when a hot- reload has occurred (config_reload_revision increments). Apply changed values without restarting.
body: -- later in update: if rt.getConfig().config_reload_revision ~= last_rev then apply() end

system: lk-sys-last-error.description: Use getLastError() after calls that can fail to show a user-facing error message and log a diagnostic hint. The table includes message, code, category, and an optional hint for resolution.

system: lk-sys-error-snapshot.description: Use errorSnapshot to create a structured JSON diagnostic that captures the engine state at the point of failure — useful for crash reporters, telemetry, and support tickets.
body: local snap = rt.errorSnapshot("unexpected nil in inventory system")

system: lk-sys-runbatch.description: Use runBatch to run a set of named boot validation tasks and collect per-task pass/fail/time results. getBatchResults summarises the counts for a single-line CI gate or dev-mode startup report.
body: for name, r in pairs(results) do

ui: lk-ui-panel-button-status.description: Use for a persistent HUD status strip with action button. Combines a panel container, a label for dynamic status text, and a button with callback — the minimal pattern for any interactive overlay widget.

ui: lk-ui-label-score.description: Use to show and update a score or counter label that changes frequently. Create once at init, call setText each frame or on event — no widget rebuild required.

ui: lk-ui-button-enable-guard.description: Use to disable action buttons during async operations, cutscenes, or cooldown periods to prevent double-activation. Re-enable when the condition clears.

ui: lk-ui-input-validate.description: Use for name entry, chat input, or any text field that must validate before submit. Reads getText(), checks constraints, and shows error label feedback without leaving the screen.

ui: lk-ui-horizontal-toolbar.description: Use to build a toolbar of buttons that auto-spaces itself. newLayout with "horizontal" direction + spacing handles positioning automatically — adding or removing buttons does not require manual coordinate updates.

ui: lk-ui-flex-sidebar.description: Use to split a panel into a fixed sidebar and a flexible content area. setFlexGrow(0) fixes the sidebar width; setFlexGrow(1) lets the content area expand to fill remaining space without hardcoding coordinates.
body: print("sidebar grow=" .. sidebar:getFlexGrow() .. " content grow=" .. content:getFlexGrow())

ui: lk-ui-grid-inventory.description: Use for inventory, hotbar, or card selection grids. newLayout with "grid" direction + setColumns handles the row wrapping — position stays correct when slots are added or removed dynamically.

ui: lk-ui-padding-margin.description: Use to add comfortable inner spacing (padding) and outer gap (margin) to any widget. setPadding controls the content box inset; setMargin controls gap to siblings in a layout container.
body: local t, r, b, l = card:getPadding()
print(string.format("padding t=%d r=%d b=%d l=%d", t, r, b, l))

ui: lk-ui-toast-fade.description: Use for transient notification toasts. The widget slides in, waits, then fades out with hide_on_complete so it is automatically hidden when done — no manual timer polling needed.
body: -- after delay: toast:animateAlpha(0.0, 0.5, true)   -- fade out and hide

ui: lk-ui-dialog-slide.description: Use for modal dialogs, pause menus, and contextual panels. slideIn brings the widget on-screen from an off-screen offset; slideOut sends it back. Pair with cancelAnimations to interrupt when the player presses ESC.
body: -- listen for animating = false, then: dialog:setVisible(false)

ui: lk-ui-tween-position.description: Use for animated card moves, ability icon repositioning, and drag-to-drop previews. animatePosition drives the widget to a world-space target over a duration; cancel returns it instantly for emergency dismissal.
body: -- cancel if player picks it back up:

ui: lk-ui-slider-volume.description: Use for any continuous setting: volume, brightness, sensitivity. Create a labeled slider; read getValue() on change callback to apply the setting to the audio bus or engine config.

ui: lk-ui-checkbox.description: Use for binary settings (fullscreen, subtitles, inverted controls). Checkbox is the clearest affordance for on/off options in settings menus; read isChecked() in the callback to apply the change immediately.
body: -- apply subtitle setting to game state

ui: lk-ui-dropdown-language.description: Use for language selection, difficulty, or any enum-style setting. Dropdown collapses to a single line when closed — good for dense settings menus where screen space is limited.
body: -- NOTE: newDropdown is not yet in the API; use newComboBox when available
-- or implement with newPanel + newButton per option.

ui: lk-ui-progress-bar.description: Use for loading screens, ability charge bars, and health or XP indicators. setValue drives the fill fraction; update the label text in sync to avoid separate string-update overhead.

ui: lk-ui-hit-test-tooltip.description: Use to show a custom tooltip when the cursor is over a widget. containsPoint maps mouse coords to widget bounds — works even for widgets not in the registered UI tree (e.g. custom-drawn items).
body: tooltip_target:setTooltip("Deals 3d6 fire damage in a 2-tile radius")

ui: lk-ui-z-order-modal.description: Use to guarantee a modal dialog always draws above all other widgets. setZOrder with a very high value (e.g. 1000) ensures it renders last, regardless of creation order.
